reasoning:
The submission is ambitious and covers many requested areas, but it is not fully reliable as a runnable, spec-complete deliverable.

For component architecture: it has a sensible high-level decomposition (App/Header/SearchBar/Board/Column/Card/CardModal), context + reducer global state, and several custom hooks. However, concerns are inconsistently separated (drag logic duplicated instead of cleanly using the dedicated hook), some hooks/components are unused or partially implemented, and there are broken patterns (e.g., dynamic require in a React component, incomplete keyboard movement logic in Card).

For TypeScript strictness: strict tsconfig and useful shared interfaces/unions are present. But there are correctness issues that would likely fail type-check/build (e.g., useBoard.getState?.(), require in ESM browser code, questionable clsx import style). There are also avoidable assertions and incomplete type-safe handling in places.

For drag-and-drop: HTML5 DnD is used directly with dragstart/dragover/drop/dragend and visible feedback (placeholder/highlight/drag styling). Cross-column move and in-column reorder are conceptually handled. But implementation has fragility: index calculation is heuristic, some target detection logic references selectors not wired in markup, and the separate useDragAndDrop hook is not integrated.

For accessibility/keyboard: there are ARIA labels/roles, live region announcements, and general keyboard operability of buttons/forms. Dark mode toggle + persistence/system preference handling is present. But key requirement of keyboard card movement (Alt+Arrow) is not actually completed/functioning, and focus management after major actions is limited.

For search/filter/undo: search by title+description is real-time; priority and tag filters are multi-select; non-matching cards are dimmed rather than removed. Undo stack exists with depth 20 and Ctrl/Cmd+Z handling, and reducer actions are wired for major operations. This area is relatively strong, though some implementation details are brittle.

For code quality/setup: file organization is close to requested structure but not fully compliant (missing requested FilterPanel and tailwind.config.ts deliverable). There are compile/runtime red flags that may prevent clean npm run dev without fixes. There is also dead/unused code and inconsistent implementation quality despite good breadth.
